notes: This overlay keeps the repository blueprint truth active while leaving continuation in explicit manual mode.
The report-production and report-delivery waves remain part of active blueprint truth.

The release-window attestation follow-up wave is now completed in repo truth through L17.4.

The feed-bundle and digest-delivery hardening wave is now fully completed in repo truth through L19.6.

This wave corrected the repo-read: DataPulse already had packs, feed exports, and digest builders, so the real gap was replayable feed_bundle semantics, deterministic digest payloads, prompt override order, shared onboarding defaults, and Telegram delivery hardening.

The L20 governance-snapshot decoupling wave is now completed in repo truth through L20.5, so resolver-owned runtime bundle roots and same-window evidence roots are no longer coupled to tracked out/* truth.
The L21 ModelBus repo-prep wave is now completed in repo truth through L21.3, preserving resolver-owned bundle-first semantics while keeping web_research non-public.

L21.1 and L21.2 are now landed in repo truth: public-surface runtime-hit and same-window attestation closure covers mission_suggest, triage_assist, claim_draft, delivery_summary, plus report_draft=verified_fail_closed.
The L22 console text-measurement hardening wave is now completed in repo truth through L22.4, leaving grapheme-safe truncation and dense single-line canvas-fit handling as the current baseline.
The L23 console text-overflow runtime-evidence wave remains completed in repo truth through L23.2, so the console-text lane itself stays closed unless a new evidence-backed text wave is opened.
The L24 console interaction-clarity wave is now completed in repo truth through L24.5.
The L25 workflow-first console simplification wave is now completed in repo truth through L25.5.
The L26 console modularity-and-traceability follow-up wave is now fully completed in repo truth through L26.5.

The current blueprint reopening target is the L27 runtime-kernel and multi-surface projection wave because repo facts now show DataPulse's next leverage point is Reader-backed runtime and surface-parity hardening rather than more collector count or another UI-only expansion.
L27.1 is now the current manual ignition target: land the runtime-kernel and surface-projection blueprint plus fact promotion, then freeze the canonical layer map before any deeper refactor work.

This reopen keeps Reader-backed lifecycle truth canonical, treats collectors as acquisition adapters, keeps AI as a governed overlay, and does not reopen shell-order simplification or force a frontend-stack migration.
Operator review should treat post-CI closeout churn as a coupling bug to remove, not as a reason to invent another parallel loop or runtime control plane.
